pattern: Walk the list once, flipping each node's next pointer to its predecessor, carrying the new head along.
hints: You can rebuild the links as you go — you do not need extra storage proportional to the list.
Keep three references as you advance: the node before, the current node, and the node after (so you don't lose the rest of the list when you flip a pointer).
Initialize prev = None; for each node, save next, point node.next back at prev, then move prev and node forward; return prev as the new head.

edge_inputs.description: duplicate values keep their multiplicity and order reverses

stress.description: long random list — confirms O(n) one-pass reversal
